title: Sensor Not Connecting
description: Fix Bluetooth pairing issues, range problems, and interference.

sections.body: Can't find your sensor during BLE scan? Here are the most common causes and fixes.

sections.heading: Check the Basics
sections.body:
1. Is the sensor powered on? Check for a blinking blue LED (BLE advertising mode). If no LED, the battery may be dead — see Battery & Charging.
  2. Is Bluetooth enabled on your phone? Go to your phone's settings and make sure Bluetooth is on.
  3. Did you grant Bluetooth permissions? On Android, both Bluetooth and Location permissions are required for BLE scanning. On iOS, Bluetooth permission is needed.

sections.heading: Range Issues
sections.body:
BLE has a typical range of 10–30 meters (30–100 feet) in open air, but walls, furniture, and other objects reduce this significantly. For best results:

  • Stay within 5 meters (15 feet) of the sensor during initial pairing.
  • Avoid placing sensors behind thick walls or metal shelves.
  • If range is a problem, consider a Solo sensor (WiFi backup) or PlantCam Pro (hub mode).

sections.heading: Interference
sections.body:
BLE operates on the 2.4 GHz band, shared with WiFi, microwaves, and other Bluetooth devices. If you have many BLE devices in close proximity, they may interfere:

  • Move the sensor away from WiFi routers and microwaves.
  • If you have many BLE devices, spread them out physically.
  • Try scanning from a different location in the room.

sections.heading: Sensor Not Appearing in Scan
sections.body:
The app scans specifically for devices advertising the Plantgotchi GATT service UUID (0000FE00-...). If your sensor doesn't appear:

  1. Power cycle the sensor (remove and reinsert battery, or press the reset button).
  2. Close and reopen the Plantgotchi app.
  3. On Android, try toggling Bluetooth off and on in system settings.
  4. Make sure no other app or device is currently connected to the sensor — BLE supports only one active connection at a time.

sections.heading: Already Paired to Another Phone
sections.body: A Plantgotchi sensor can only be actively connected to one device at a time. If another phone is connected, the sensor won't appear in your scan. Disconnect the other phone first, then scan again. Note: multiple phones can receive BLE advertisements simultaneously, but only one can hold an active GATT connection.
